content: Just as the English language understanding of reflexion as both 'reflection of light' and 'deliberate self-review' maps directly to a missing capability in existing LLM self-reflection mechanisms, we can build a dual-mode LLM reflexion framework that combines the passive 'mirroring' of external environmental feedback (the light reflection sense) and active iterative self-critique (the self-reflection sense) to improve reasoning performance. This framework leverages the dual etymological meaning of reflexion to resolve a key limitation of current LLM self-reflection approaches that often ignore passive capture of inconsistent external feedback.
